name: PSMA-617-DOTA-Lu177
label: approved
notes: Lutetium vipivotide tetraxetan (Pluvicto). FDA approved March 2022 for mCRPC. First PSMA-targeted radioligand therapy approved in the US.

name: DOTATATE-DOTA-Lu177
label: approved
notes: Lutetium dotatate (Lutathera). FDA approved January 2018 for GEP-NETs. First approved peptide receptor radionuclide therapy (PRRT).

name: MIBG-I131-direct
label: approved
notes: Iobenguane I-131 (Azedra). FDA approved May 2018 for pheochromocytoma/paraganglioma. Direct iodination — no metal chelator required.

name: DOTATOC-DOTA-Y90
label: clinical
notes: Yttrium-90 DOTATOC (SomaKit TOC). EMA approved for NET imaging/therapy. In clinical use in Europe. Y-90 half-life (2.67d) shorter than Lu-177.

name: PSMA-I&T-DOTAGA-Lu177
label: clinical
notes: PSMA-I&T with DOTAGA chelator. Multiple ongoing Phase II/III trials for mCRPC. Strong clinical evidence but not yet FDA approved as of knowledge cutoff.

name: FAPI-46-DOTA-Lu177
label: clinical
notes: FAPI-46 labeled with Lu-177. Multiple clinical trials across various solid tumors. FAP expressed in tumor stroma.

name: RGD-NOTA-Ga68

targeting_vector.name: RGD-peptide
targeting_vector.notes: RGD cyclic peptide targeting integrin alpha-v-beta-3

label: clinical
notes: Ga-68 RGD peptide. Clinical imaging trials for tumor angiogenesis. Imaging agent (Ga-68 not suitable for therapy due to short half-life).

name: Unknown1-NOTA-Lu177

targeting_vector.name: UnknownVector-X
targeting_vector.notes: ILLUSTRATIVE PLACEHOLDER — fictional compound for benchmark purposes only

label: failed
notes: ILLUSTRATIVE PLACEHOLDER — fictional compound for benchmark purposes only. Represents a poor candidate: NOTA is unstable with Lu-177 (known chelation incompatibility) and target is unknown.

name: Unknown2-DOTA-Ga68-therapy-attempt

targeting_vector.name: UnknownVector-Y
targeting_vector.notes: ILLUSTRATIVE PLACEHOLDER — fictional compound for benchmark purposes only

label: failed
notes: ILLUSTRATIVE PLACEHOLDER — fictional compound for benchmark purposes only. Represents a poor candidate: Ga-68 half-life (68 min) is far too short for therapy, suitable only for imaging. VEGFR targeting poorly validated.
